behaviors.description: The evaluate-stage command stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is command-to-artifact-store handoff (module interaction), not single-function logic. PROOF-BUNDLE (Integration vs Agentic): forcing phrase is "stores grounding into a dedicated `grounding` artifact, not the `plan` artifact"; this is storage routing correctness, not model judgment quality. Adjacent-lower miss: a Unit test on the selector function can pass while real command wiring still writes to the wrong slot due to adapter/config mismatch. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess required; correctness does not depend on LLM output quality; no multi-agentic-step chain required. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is about artifact target slot, not evaluating model-produced content.
behaviors.test_description: Run evaluate-stage command with an in-process run context and artifact store; assert a `grounding` artifact is created/updated and `plan` is unchanged by that write path.

behaviors.description: The implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`.
behaviors.justification: This is read/write orchestration across implement flow + artifact store. PROOF-BUNDLE (Integration vs Agentic): forcing phrase is "reads grounding when available and still writes its execution output to `plan`"; requirement is data plumbing and destination separation, not quality of generated output. Adjacent-lower miss: Unit tests for read helper and write helper independently can pass while combined flow drops grounding or writes to wrong artifact key. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess; no assertion on model decision quality; single stage flow, not multi-agentic pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text does not require validating what the model decided, only that grounding is consumed and output is stored to `plan`.
behaviors.test_description: Seed run context with a `grounding` artifact, execute implement flow with deterministic stubbed stage output, assert grounding is read (e.g., appears in inputs/context) and final write goes to `plan` without mutating `grounding`.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content.
behaviors.justification: Real risk is interaction between issue-content precondition and artifact-write side effects. PROOF-BUNDLE (Integration vs Agentic): forcing phrase is "no longer overwrites `plan` as a redundant side effect" conditioned on diagnosis in issue content; this is conditional side-effect control, not model-output correctness. Adjacent-lower miss: Unit test for condition parser can pass while deep-dive executor still performs unconditional `plan` write due to integration-layer default behavior. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no OS/network/subprocess dependency; no dependence on AI output quality; no multi-step chain needed. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text scopes to suppressing a write side effect, not judging model reasoning quality.
behaviors.test_description: Provide issue input already containing diagnosis, run deep-dive stage in an in-process pipeline, assert no `plan` artifact overwrite event occurs (or prior `plan` remains intact).

behaviors.description: Artifact lineage is preserved so implement can read the `grounding` artifact produced earlier in the same run context.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at run-context lineage/state propagation boundary across stages. PROOF-BUNDLE (Integration vs Agentic): forcing phrase is "lineage is preserved" and "produced earlier in the same run context"; this validates state continuity, not whether model output is good. Adjacent-lower miss: Unit tests for artifact lookup pass, but lineage metadata or context keying mismatch across modules prevents implement from seeing prior grounding. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement; correctness does not depend on model judgment; does not require multiple real model decisions to assert lineage linkage. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow/Agentic because behavior text demands cross-stage artifact visibility, not model-decision quality across agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Execute evaluate then implement within one in-process run context using real context/artifact modules; assert implement resolves the exact `grounding` artifact ID/version produced by evaluate.

behaviors.description: In a full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`), the final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a full chained agent pipeline with multiple steps and cross-step influence on final output. MULTI-STEP trigger: "full multi-step agent run (`deep-dive -> write-plan/evaluate -> implement`)". LLM-DEP trigger: "final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier" depends on model-mediated use of earlier grounding in later generation. Adjacent-lower miss: an Agentic single-step test can show model responds to grounding, but misses failures in handoff/order across deep-dive→evaluate→implement sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real end-to-end chain in one run context (deep-dive, evaluate, implement) and assert the final `plan` contains implementation decisions traceable to earlier `grounding` artifact content from the same run.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts.
behaviors.justification: This is fallback-state handling across implement flow and artifact store consistency. PROOF-BUNDLE (Integration vs Agentic): forcing phrase is "falls back safely" and "still emits a valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts"; this is robustness of orchestration/state mutation, not model output quality. Adjacent-lower miss: Unit fallback logic can pass while integrated artifact transaction/order corrupts sibling artifacts under no-grounding state. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no OS/network/subprocess need; no correctness dependence on AI quality; no required multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text focuses on artifact safety/integrity under missing input, not judging model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run implement in a context with no `grounding` artifact and with pre-existing unrelated artifacts; assert a valid `plan` is produced and unrelated artifacts remain byte-for-byte/metadata unchanged.
